Strip Permissions-Policy, Origin-Agent-Cluster, Via headers

- Permissions-Policy / Feature-Policy: restricted browser APIs
  (camera, mic, geolocation) for the entire localhost origin,
  affecting every proxied site simultaneously.  Strip both variants.
- Origin-Agent-Cluster: ?1 isolates agent cluster, preventing
  cross-window interactions needed in multi-page proxy flows.
- Via (response): discloses internal CDN/proxy topology to clients.
- Via (request): stripped in director to avoid leaking proxy metadata
  to upstream servers.

Adds TestPermissionsPolicyStripped, TestOriginAgentClusterStripped,
TestViaHeaderStripped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reduce toolchain drift across Dockerfile, mise, and pyproject (#87)

Closes #80

## Summary

Pins all floating versions, adds Renovate tracking, tightens Python
version policy, and adds drift detection to CI.

### Version pins

| Component | Before | After |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Python base image | `python:3.14-slim` | `python:3.14.4-slim` |
| gh CLI | `apt-get install gh` (floating) | Binary download, `ARG
GH_VERSION=2.89.0` |
| mise.docker.toml tools | `latest` | `shellcheck=0.11.0,
actionlint=1.7.12, trivy=0.69.3` |
| Workflow runners | `ubuntu-latest` | `ubuntu-24.04` |
| Python policy | `>=3.12` / `py312` | `>=3.14` / `py314` |

### Renovate tracking

All Dockerfile ARGs now have `# renovate:` datasource comments
(`jdx/mise`, `github/copilot-cli`, `cli/cli`). A `customManagers` regex
in `renovate.json` matches these for automatic update PRs.

### Drift detection

`scripts/check-versions.sh` validates Python version consistency across
`Dockerfile`, `mise.toml`, and both `pyproject.toml` files. Wired into
`mise run ci` via the `check:versions` task — CI fails if versions
diverge.

### Agent guardrails

- **SKILL.md**: Added guidance to implement what's possible and list
restricted items (e.g., workflow edits) in the PR description instead of
failing
- **write-issue skill**: Added guidance to note workflow changes as
out-of-scope for the agent

---------

commit.message:
Harden pre-generation environment audit and refactor into `--env-audit` command (#3863)

* Self threat model improvements

Signed-off-by: Prabhu Subramanian <prabhu@appthreat.com>

* Improve auditEnvironment and displaySelfThreatModel for production readiness

- Fix NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED: only flag when value is "0" (critical),
  not on any non-empty value
- Remove --test from CODE_EXECUTION_PATTERNS (built-in test runner, not
  an exploit vector); note in comment
- Tighten short-flag regexes (drop \b-r\b and \b-e\b false-positive paths)
- Add JVM code-injection audit for JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS / JDK_JAVA_OPTIONS
  (-javaagent, -agentlib, -agentpath, --add-opens, etc.)
- Add proxy interception detection (HTTP_PROXY / HTTPS_PROXY / lowercase)
- Add credential exposure detection (GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, NPM_AUTH_TOKEN,
  PYPI_TOKEN, CDXGEN_API_KEY)
- Add running-as-root check (process.getuid() === 0)
- Add CDXGEN_DEBUG_MODE / SCAN_DEBUG_MODE=debug verbose logging warning
- Expand per-variable messages in RISKY_PRESENCE_VARS to explain each risk
- Improve all mitigation texts for actionability

displaySelfThreatModel:
- Rename title "cdxgen Threat Model Report" -> "SBOM Generation Environment
  Assessment" with subtitle "Pre-generation security audit by cdxgen"
- Rewrite all finding descriptions (formulation, evidence mode, auto-install,
  TLS, config file, remote submission) for clarity
- Restore output-path-outside-CWD check (was dropped in refactor)
- Replace double-counting of CDXGEN_INCLUDE_FORMULATION/CDXGEN_API_KEY
  (already covered by env-audit) with a single CDXGEN_SERVER_URL env check
- Align riskLevel thresholds: HIGH at >=5 (was inconsistently >=6)
- Use descriptive category names: configuration/network/data-exposure/
  environment/runtime/privilege instead of config/remote/data
- Add Profile and Recursive rows to Effective Configuration table
- Rename Type(s) / Output columns, default output to "(stdout)"
- Improve recommendations: more actionable wording, language-agnostic
  hardening example, add privilege and JVM remediations
- Tighten secure-mode abort message

Tests:
- Update hasSuspiciousWarning substring to match new message wording
- Add test cases for --test (safe), NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0/1,
  JVM code-execution patterns, proxy detection, credential exposure,
  and debug-mode exposure (57 tests, all pass)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/cdxgen/cdxgen/sessions/a9f9a80c-1bf7-4895-98d5-c22957c95d5c

Co-authored-by: prabhu <7842+prabhu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: pre-compile permission regexes, fix output-path check, fix spelling

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/cdxgen/cdxgen/sessions/a9f9a80c-1bf7-4895-98d5-c22957c95d5c

Co-authored-by: prabhu <7842+prabhu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: refactor display, rename --env-audit, tweak severities and credential detection

- Move displaySelfThreatModel to display.js (env-audit.js now only returns data, no table import)
- displaySelfThreatModel calls auditEnvironment() internally; bin/cdxgen.js no longer threads envAuditFindings to it
- Rename CLI flag --threat-model → --env-audit (hidden); update call site to options.envAudit
- Downgrade NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED severity: critical → high (legitimate env var, not injection)
- Downgrade proxy finding severity: medium → low (informational; common in enterprise networks)
- Replace hardcoded CREDENTIAL_VARS list with CREDENTIAL_VAR_PATTERN regex matching any env var
  whose name ends with _TOKEN, _KEY, _SECRET, _PASS, _PASSWORD, _CREDENTIAL(S); severity low
- Add CDXGEN_IN_CONTAINER !== 'true' guard to root check (official container images run as root by design)
- Update tests: TLS severity high, credential tests updated for pattern-based detection (59 tests pass)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/cdxgen/cdxgen/sessions/6ec59f4b-d197-443c-9381-99871ade09bf

Co-authored-by: prabhu <7842+prabhu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tweaks. Fixes test

Signed-off-by: Prabhu Subramanian <prabhu@appthreat.com>

* Fix CI failure: add $ anchor to CREDENTIAL_VAR_PATTERN; only exit secure mode for high/critical findings

SSH_AUTH_SOCK (ends with _SOCK) and __CF_USER_TEXT_ENCODING (ends with _ENCODING) were
matched as credentials by the missing end-anchor, causing process.exit(1) in the
secure-mode repotests CI job.

- Add end-of-string anchor to CREDENTIAL_VAR_PATTERN so it only fires when the
  credential keyword IS the trailing segment of the variable name, not mid-name
  (e.g. SSH_AUTH_SOCK contains _AUTH but ends with _SOCK → no match)
- credential(?:s)? replaces the bare `cred` alternative so that SVC_CREDENTIAL
  and CLOUD_CREDENTIALS still match correctly
- Restrict bin/cdxgen.js secure-mode exit to high/critical severity findings only;
  low/medium findings (debug-exposure, proxy, credential-exposure) print a warning
  but must not abort a CI scan
- Add regression test for SSH_AUTH_SOCK and __CF_USER_TEXT_ENCODING not being flagged

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/cdxgen/cdxgen/sessions/a98dd3b1-768a-41b9-aa48-291273f15c0d

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/cdxgen/cdxgen/sessions/a98dd3b1-768a-41b9-aa48-291273f15c0d

Co-authored-by: prabhu <7842+prabhu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Regen map files

Signed-off-by: Prabhu Subramanian <prabhu@appthreat.com>

* Add Deno permission support to auditEnvironment: DENO_CERT and Deno.permissions.querySync checks

- DENO_CERT env var: flags a custom TLS CA at severity "high" (analogous to
  NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 for Node.js — enables MITM with a proxy)
- Deno live permission model: when running under Deno, uses
  Deno.permissions.querySync to detect whether unrestricted shell execution
  (sh/bash on Unix, cmd/powershell on Windows) is granted — a strong signal
  that --allow-all or --allow-run without restrictions was used; any such
  finding is severity "high" with actionable mitigation
- Wrapped in try/catch so future Deno versions that remove/change querySync
  don't break the audit
- 8 new tests covering DENO_CERT present/empty/absent, Unix and Windows
  shell-allowed paths, restricted allow-run (no false positive), querySync
  throw (silent skip), and Node.js baseline (no false positive)

fix: handle warning stream content during OOP server startup (#84)

When PowerShell modules (e.g., Az) emit Write-Warning during Import-Module
the output leaks to stdout in the OOP subprocess, corrupting the ndjson
protocol channel and producing alarming log entries.

Changes:
- oop-host.ps1: Add -WarningAction SilentlyContinue/-WarningVariable to all
  Import-Module and Install-Module calls so warnings go to stderr via
  Write-Diag instead of stdout.
- OutOfProcessCommandExecutor.cs: Add IsNonJsonPowerShellStreamLine() helper
  that detects WARNING:/VERBOSE:/DEBUG:/INFORMATION:/ERROR: prefixes and
  skips them gracefully at Debug log level instead of emitting a Warning with
  a full exception stack trace.
- Demote the catch(JsonException) log from LogWarning(ex,...) to LogDebug to
  remove alarm fatigue for any remaining unexpected non-JSON content.

Fixes #78

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Strip __Host-/__Secure- cookie prefixes when Secure flag is removed

When the proxy listens on plain HTTP, it strips the Secure flag from
Set-Cookie headers. However, cookies whose names begin with __Host- or
__Secure- also require the Secure attribute — without it browsers
silently reject the cookie, breaking any session that relies on them.

Fix: strip the prefix from the cookie name before stripping Secure.
This preserves the session value at the cost of the browser-enforced
security guarantee, which is acceptable in a local proxy/CTF context.

Tests: added three new sub-cases to TestRewriteSetCookiesUnit covering
  __Host- stripped (plain HTTP), __Secure- stripped (plain HTTP),
  __Host- kept (HTTPS proxy).

Also updated go-rewriting-reverse-proxy skill with new pitfalls:
SRI integrity stripping, __Host-/__Secure- cookies, Service-Worker-Allowed,
Vary: Accept-Encoding cleanup, protocol-relative URL rewriting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Strip Link header integrity params and Service-Worker-Allowed header

Link header integrity:
  Link: </app.js>; rel=preload; integrity=sha256-xxx
  After proxy rewrites the resource, the precomputed hash is stale.
  Strip '; integrity=...' params from all Link header values so the
  browser doesn't block the preloaded (potentially rewritten) bytes.
  Added linkIntegrityRe and TestLinkHeaderIntegrityStripped.

Service-Worker-Allowed:
  A service worker registered via /__sd__/<host>/sw.js could set
  Service-Worker-Allowed: / and claim the entire proxy origin,
  intercepting all /__sd__/ routes. Strip this header to limit SW
  scope to the path it was served from.
  Added TestServiceWorkerAllowedStripped.

Strip SRI integrity attributes from proxied HTML responses

  may differ from what the upstream hash was computed against

commit.message:
feat: HTTPS support with automatic protocol discovery for RainBird v2 controllers (#45)

RainBird v2 firmware controllers use HTTPS with self-signed certificates
instead of plain HTTP. Without this change, the library fails to
communicate with any controller updated to the v2 firmware. See the
equivalent fix in the Python ecosystem:
[home-assistant/core#163915](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/163915)
(pyrainbird v6.1.0).

**Changes Proposed in this Pull Request**

- **Protocol discovery on first request** (`RainBirdClient`): Probes
`https://{host}/stick` first; falls back to `http://{host}/stick` on
transport/TLS failure (e.g. `ECONNRESET`,
`ERR_SSL_WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER`). HTTP-level errors (403, 503) do not
trigger a fallback — HTTPS is correctly retained.
- **Self-signed cert acceptance**: Shared `Agent` with
`rejectUnauthorized: false` is used for HTTPS. This is a necessary
trade-off for local LAN devices with device-generated certificates;
documented in code comments.
- **Maintainable error classification**: Transport errors are matched
via a static `ReadonlySet` of error codes plus a `readonly` array of
message substrings, keeping the logic easy to extend.
- **Version bump**: `1.2.8` → `1.3.0`

```typescript
// First request probes HTTPS automatically — no API change required.
// Legacy HTTP controllers: HTTPS probe fails → falls back transparently.
// RainBird v2 controllers: HTTPS probe succeeds → all subsequent requests use HTTPS.
const service = new RainBirdService({ address, password, ... })
await service.init() // discovery happens here on getModelAndVersion()
```

**Version Impact**

- [ ] `patch` - Bug fixes and small improvements
- [x] `minor` - New features or enhancements
- [ ] `major` - Breaking changes

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Exposing an explicit `useHttps` flag in `RainBirdService` options was
considered, but automatic discovery is strictly better: it's
backward-compatible, requires no user configuration change, and matches
the pyrainbird v6.1.0 approach.

**Additional context**

The discovery is done once per `RainBirdClient` instance and the result
is cached in `_url`. Since the `PQueue` enforces concurrency=1, there is
no race condition during discovery.

---------

commit.message:
Handle int overflow in rnn (#28003)

### Description

Fixes two overflow/underflow bugs in the CPU RNN kernel (`rnn.cc`):

- **`SafeInt` for GEMM M-dimension**: `seq_length * batch_size` was
computed as a raw `int64_t` multiply before `narrow<int>()`, meaning an
overflow would be UB before the check could fire. Replaced with
`SafeInt<int64_t>(seq_length) * batch_size` for a checked multiply.

- **`seq_length == 0` guard in `Assign_Y_h`**: For the forward
direction, `last_time_step = seq_length - 1` underflows to `-1` when
`seq_length == 0`, producing a negative `y_offset` and out-of-bounds
read. Added an early-exit that zero-fills Y_h for the direction and
returns. Also handles `sequence_lens[batch] == 0` (same underflow path),
zeroing the affected batch slot and skipping via `continue`.

### Motivation and Context

Silent UB from integer overflow/underflow in shape-derived index
arithmetic can corrupt memory or produce incorrect results without any
diagnostic signal. These cases are legal per the ONNX spec (empty
sequences, per-batch zero-length sequences) and must be handled
explicitly.

commit.message:
Consolidate file path resolution for WebView controls (#34620)

<!-- Please let the below note in for people that find this PR -->
> [!NOTE]
> Are you waiting for the changes in this PR to be merged?
> It would be very helpful if you could [test the resulting
artifacts](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Testing-PR-Builds) from
this PR and let us know in a comment if this change resolves your issue.
Thank you!

## Description

Refactored scattered file-path resolution logic in HybridWebView and
BlazorWebView into shared utilities (`FileSystemUtils.Combine`,
`WebUtils.ResolveRelativePath`) to fix edge cases where `Path.Combine`
drops the root directory when a relative path starts with a separator.

### Changes

- **`FileSystemUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `IsValidRelativePath()` and
`Combine()` methods that validate relative paths and ensure the resolved
path stays within the expected root directory
- **`WebUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `ResolveRelativePath()` to resolve
request URIs against an app origin into validated relative paths
- **HybridWebView handlers** (iOS, Windows, Android) — Use the new
shared utilities for file path resolution
- **BlazorWebView file providers** (iOS, Android, Windows, Tizen) — Use
`FileSystemUtils.Combine()` for path resolution
- **`Email.windows.cs`** — Consolidated duplicate `NormalizePath` into
`FileSystemUtils.NormalizePath`
- **Device tests** — Added path resolution tests for both HybridWebView
and BlazorWebView covering relative paths, rooted paths, dot-dot
segments, and encoded separators

Fix sprite batch lighting bounds accumulation

Box3_Union was accumulating in->bounds (the first sprite) instead of
batch->bounds (the current sprite), so R_ActiveLights only considered
the first sprite's position for the entire batch. When that sprite was
far from an explosion, the lighting query found no lights, driving
diffuse to zero and making lit sprites invisible under additive
blending. Since particle order varies per frame, this caused flickering.

commit.message:
fix: prevent 304 bypass of body rewriting via conditional request caching

Two related bugs fixed:

1. Outbound conditional request headers not stripped
   The proxy was forwarding If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Match,
   If-Unmodified-Since, and If-Range to the upstream unchanged.  When the
   browser had a cached copy from a previous proxy run and resent these headers,
   the upstream could return 304 Not Modified.  ModifyResponse's noBody path
   skips all body rewriting on 304 responses, so the browser would then display
   its stale cached copy — which may contain unrewritten upstream hostnames.
   Fix: strip all five conditional headers in the Director before forwarding.

2. ETag + Last-Modified response headers not stripped
   By caching these validators the browser would eventually send conditional
   requests (see bug 1).  Strip both in headersStrip so the browser never has
   a validator to send for proxied resources.

Add TestConditionalRequestHeadersStripped and TestETagLastModifiedStripped.
